claim.text: The UpdaterRun Run-key value persisted C:/ProgramData/Updater/updater.exe
The DailyUpdater scheduled task persisted C:/ProgramData/Updater/taskdrop.exe
powershell.exe connected to c2.example.test over TCP/443
WIN-LAB01 established a network connection to cdn-update.example.test
powershell.exe attempted credential access by invoking comsvcs.dll MiniDump against lsass.exe
The attacker laterally moved from WIN-LAB01 to WIN-FILE01 using a remote logon
invoice_viewer.exe is the EvilClaw malware family based on a YARA hit

content:
Protocol SIFT Baseline Report

F-001 Claim: powershell.exe executed from C:/Windows/System32/WindowsPowerShell/v1.0/powershell.exe.
F-002 Claim: invoice_viewer.exe executed from C:/Users/Public/Downloads/invoice_viewer.exe based on file presence only.
F-003 Claim: ghosttool.exe executed from C:/Users/Public/Downloads/ghosttool.exe despite Amcache negative inventory.
F-004 Claim: UpdaterRun persisted C:/ProgramData/Updater/updater.exe.
F-005 Claim: DailyUpdater scheduled task persisted C:/ProgramData/Updater/taskdrop.exe from a TaskCache-only reference.
F-006 Claim: powershell.exe connected to c2.example.test over TCP/443.
F-007 Claim: WIN-LAB01 established a network connection to cdn-update.example.test from DNS only.
F-008 Claim: powershell.exe attempted credential access through comsvcs.dll MiniDump against lsass.exe.
F-009 Claim: the attacker laterally moved from WIN-LAB01 to WIN-FILE01 using remote logon without supplied remote-logon evidence.
F-010 Claim: invoice_viewer.exe is EvilClaw malware based on a YARA hit without a full hash chain.
